rag_rules: Preserve owner/scope boundaries: place type-level contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings only in implementation scopes.
When merging annex content, keep references limited to names visible in the chosen owner scope; do not introduce subcomponent paths into a type-owned contract.
Preserve surrounding AADL architecture by adding annex content without changing existing subcomponents, connections, features, properties, or ownership structure.
generic_rules: Insert new contract content into the correct owning declaration and keep existing declarations intact.
If a construct depends on implementation details, keep it with the implementation owner rather than moving it to the type.
Use only concrete, model-visible identifiers and bounds supplied by upstream artifacts; avoid inventing architecture elements during fusion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Component types normally own assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementations normally own implementation-level bindings such as assignments when valid in that scope.
anti_patterns: Do not relocate implementation-specific content into a component type scope.
Do not alter AADL architecture or ownership just to accommodate annex insertion.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as in-scope AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-specific type-repair tactics are not needed when performing owner/scope-preserving fusion.
Detailed requirement semantics are upstream concerns unless they affect owner scope or visible-name preservation.
